Body Fat Percentage Calculator (Canada)

✅ US Navy Circumference Method  |  ✅ Accurate to ±3-4% of DEXA  |  ✅ Dietitian-Reviewed 2026

Use our free body fat percentage calculator to instantly estimate how much of your body weight is fat tissue. Based on the US Navy circumference method — accurate to within 3-4% of a DEXA scan, no equipment required.

Healthy Body Fat Percentage Ranges

What does your result mean? Use these clinically established ranges to interpret your body fat percentage:

Women — Body Fat Percentage Chart

Category Body Fat % What It Means
Essential Fat10–13%Minimum for hormonal health
Athletic14–20%Competitive athlete level
Fitness21–24%Active, healthy body composition
Acceptable25–31%Healthy, room for improvement
Obese32%+Associated with metabolic risk

Men — Body Fat Percentage Chart

Category Body Fat % What It Means
Essential Fat2–5%Minimum for organ function
Athletic6–13%Competitive athlete level
Fitness14–17%Active, healthy body composition
Acceptable18–24%Healthy, room for improvement
Obese25%+Associated with metabolic risk

How to Calculate Body Fat Percentage — Step by Step

The US Navy body fat formula uses circumference measurements to estimate body fat percentage. Here is how to calculate it manually:

For Men

  1. Measure your waist at the narrowest point (typically at the navel level).
  2. Measure your neck below the larynx, sloping slightly downward.
  3. Apply the formula:
Body Fat % = 495 ÷ (1.0324 − 0.19077 × log(waist − neck) + 0.15456 × log(height)) − 450

For Women

  1. Measure your waist at the narrowest point.
  2. Measure your hips at the widest point.
  3. Measure your neck below the larynx.
  4. Apply the formula:
Body Fat % = 495 ÷ (1.29579 − 0.35004 × log(waist + hip − neck) + 0.22100 × log(height)) − 450

All measurements in inches; height in inches.

Measurement Methods Compared

There are several ways to calculate body fat percentage. Here is how they compare:

Method Accuracy Cost Equipment
US Navy (this tool)±3–4%FreeTape measure
YMCA Formula±4–5%FreeTape measure
Skinfold Calipers±3–5%$10–30Calipers + skill
Bioelectrical Impedance (BIA)±3–8%$20–200BIA scale
DEXA Scan (gold standard)±1–2%$50–150Medical clinic
Hydrostatic Weighing±1–3%$50–100Specialist lab

For most people, the US Navy circumference method provides the best trade-off between accuracy, cost, and convenience — which is why it is used by the US military for mandatory fitness testing.

Body Fat vs. BMI: Why Body Fat Percentage Is More Accurate

BMI (Body Mass Index) is calculated from height and weight alone — it cannot distinguish fat from muscle. A muscular athlete and an overweight person can have the identical BMI despite completely different body compositions. Body fat percentage is a far superior metric for tracking health and fat loss progress.

  • BMI misclassifies roughly 1 in 4 people as healthy when they have high body fat ("skinny fat")
  • Body fat percentage accurately captures body composition changes even when scale weight stays the same
  • Fat loss with muscle gain (body recomposition) is invisible to BMI but visible in body fat %

Use our BMI Calculator alongside body fat percentage for a complete health picture.

How to Lower Your Body Fat Percentage

Reducing body fat while preserving muscle mass requires a two-pronged approach:

1. Create a Calorie Deficit

Fat loss only occurs when you consume fewer calories than you burn. A 300–500 calorie daily deficit is sustainable without significant muscle loss. Use our Calorie Deficit Calculator or TDEE Calculator to find your exact number.

2. Maintain High Protein Intake

Protein is the key lever for preserving lean muscle during fat loss. Research consistently shows that 0.7–1.0g of protein per pound of body weight minimizes muscle breakdown during caloric restriction. Use our Protein Calculator for your personalized target.

3. Resistance Training

Lifting weights 3–4 times per week is the most effective way to preserve (or build) muscle while losing fat. This directly improves your body fat percentage even if your scale weight changes minimally.

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I calculate my body fat percentage?

The easiest free method is the US Navy circumference method (used by this calculator). For men: measure waist and neck. For women: measure waist, hip, and neck. Enter the measurements above for an instant result accurate to ±3-4%.

What is a healthy body fat percentage for women?

For women, a fitness range of 21–24% is considered healthy and active. The acceptable healthy range extends to 25–31%. Below 10% is the essential fat threshold — going below this risks hormonal disruption.

What is a healthy body fat percentage for men?

For men, a fitness range of 14–17% is considered healthy. The acceptable healthy range extends to 18–24%. The essential fat minimum for men is 2–5%; below this risks severe metabolic and hormonal disruption.

Is the US Navy body fat method accurate?

Yes. Published studies show the US Navy method is accurate within 3–4% of DEXA scans — the clinical gold standard for body composition. It is the method the US military uses for mandatory fitness testing and is significantly more accurate than BMI alone.

How often should I measure my body fat percentage?

Measure every 4–6 weeks. Body fat changes slowly — daily or weekly measurements show too much noise from water retention, time of day, and food intake. Consistent timing (same time of day, same conditions) gives the most comparable readings.

What is the difference between body fat percentage and BMI?

BMI only uses height and weight — it cannot distinguish muscle from fat. A muscular person and a sedentary person can have the same BMI. Body fat percentage directly measures the proportion of fat tissue in your body, making it far more accurate for tracking body composition and health risk.

What body fat percentage should I aim for to lose weight?

If your goal is weight loss, aim to reduce body fat by 1% per month — a rate that preserves muscle mass. Track progress with this calculator every 4–6 weeks. Use the weight loss calculator to set your calorie target and the protein calculator to protect muscle.

Can I lose fat and gain muscle at the same time?

Yes — this is called body recomposition. It is most effective for beginners and people returning after a break. The scale may not move, but your body fat percentage will decrease as muscle replaces fat. This is why tracking body fat % is more informative than just tracking scale weight.

Medically Reviewed By

Dr. Sarah Jenkins, RD — Clinical Dietitian & Body Composition Specialist

Dr. Jenkins has over 15 years of experience in clinical nutrition and metabolic health, specializing in evidence-based body composition assessment and weight management strategies.

Last reviewed: July 2026  |  Sources: US Navy Body Fat Study (NIH)  ·  ACE Fitness Body Fat Guidelines
